title: Bengaluru AI Builders — Agents in Production
description: Monthly meetup for engineers shipping AI agents. Three lightning talks from teams running agents in production, followed by an open Q&A and demo room. Food and drinks sponsored. Bring a laptop if you want to demo.
whyAttend: Hear from engineers actually running AI agents in production — not just demos.
comfortNote: Friendly for first-timers — most people come alone too.

title: HackBLR 2026 — Bengaluru's Biggest Student Hackathon
description: 36-hour in-person hackathon. Tracks: AI agents, dev tools, climate tech, and open innovation. ₹5L in prizes, mentors from top startups, and free accommodation for out-of-city teams.
whyAttend: Build something real in 36 hours, meet mentors from top startups, win ₹5L in prizes.
comfortNote: Solo? Team-matching happens on day zero — you won't stay alone for long.

title: React India 2026
description: India's largest React conference. Two days, 24 speakers, three tracks covering Server Components, React Native, performance, and DX tooling. Workshops on day zero. Students get 40% off with a valid ID.
whyAttend: India's biggest React event — 24 speakers across 2 days. 40% off for students.
comfortNote: Small, casual crowd — easy to start a conversation.

title: PyData Bengaluru — Vector DBs & RAG at Scale
description: Talks on pgvector vs. Qdrant vs. Weaviate in production, building retrieval pipelines that actually work, and evaluation tooling for RAG systems.
whyAttend: Real-world comparisons of vector DBs from people running them in production.
comfortNote: Friendly for first-timers — most people come alone too.

title: ETHIndia Hyderabad — Hacker House
description: One-week hacker house for builders working on Ethereum and L2s. Daily workshops, co-working space, and demo day on Friday. Travel grants available for students outside Hyderabad.
whyAttend: Week-long immersion with serious Ethereum builders — rare opportunity in India.
comfortNote: Team-matching on day zero — come solo, leave with teammates.

title: MLH Global Hack Week: AI
description: A week of online AI-themed workshops, challenges, and mini-hacks run by Major League Hacking. Open to students worldwide. Daily sessions start 9 PM IST.
whyAttend: Global hackathon week you can do from your room. Swag + certificate for completing 3 workshops.
comfortNote: Team-matching on day zero — come solo, leave with teammates.

title: AWS Community Day Hyderabad 2026
description: Full-day community-led conference on AWS services. Tracks on serverless, data engineering, gen-AI on Bedrock, and cost optimization. Lunch included.
whyAttend: Free full-day AWS conference with lunch and swag — rare combination.
comfortNote: Small, casual crowd — easy to start a conversation.

title: IndiaFOSS 2026
description: India's flagship free and open source software conference. Three tracks, 40+ talks, open hacking space, and focused unconference sessions.
whyAttend: The biggest FOSS gathering in India. Meet core maintainers of tools you use every day.
comfortNote: Small, casual crowd — easy to start a conversation.

title: Design Jam Bengaluru — Redesign a Govt Website
description: Informal 4-hour design jam. Pick any Indian government service website, redesign a single user flow end to end, present in 3 minutes.
whyAttend: Leave with a real redesign for your portfolio in 4 hours.
perks: Best redesign gets a Figma Pro account
comfortNote: Come alone — you'll be paired with a small team for the jam, instant friends.

title: Rootconf 2026 — Infra & SRE
description: India's infrastructure and SRE conference. Keynotes, deep-dive talks on Kubernetes at scale, incident response, platform engineering, and cost optimization.
whyAttend: The best SRE/platform engineering talks in India. Every senior infra engineer is here.
comfortNote: Small, casual crowd — easy to start a conversation.

title: Unstop Product Case Challenge — Flipkart Edition
description: Case competition for undergrad and grad students. Submit a product strategy deck for a given Flipkart growth problem. Shortlisted teams present to a panel of Flipkart PMs.
whyAttend: Top teams get pre-placement interview offers at Flipkart. Serious career leverage.
comfortNote: Virtual, low-pressure — turn camera off if you want.

title: Startup Grind Delhi — Fireside with Nithin Kamath
description: Founders' fireside chat with Nithin Kamath (Zerodha) on building patient businesses in India. 90-minute Q&A, followed by a founder mixer.
whyAttend: Ninety uninterrupted minutes with Nithin Kamath. He rarely does public events.
comfortNote: Friendly for first-timers — most people come alone too.

title: Next.js Global Summit (Virtual)
description: One-day online summit on Next.js 16+: Cache Components, Turbopack in production, and React Server Components patterns.
whyAttend: Deep Next.js 16 internals straight from the Vercel team, free.
comfortNote: Virtual, low-pressure — turn camera off if you want.

title: Rust Bengaluru — WebAssembly Deep Dive
description: Two talks: compiling Rust to WASM for browser-side ML, and wasmtime in production. Monthly community meetup.
whyAttend: Advanced WASM content rare in India — both speakers are shipping it in production.
comfortNote: Hallway track is the real event — most attendees come solo.

title: BlrDroid — Android & Kotlin Monthly
description: Three talks on Jetpack Compose performance, Kotlin Multiplatform for cross-platform teams, and debugging ANRs at scale.
whyAttend: Bengaluru's oldest Android community — senior engineers from Swiggy, Flipkart, Google show up.
comfortNote: Hallway track is the real event — most attendees come solo.

title: Nullcon Mumbai — Offensive Security
description: Three-day security conference with red team, AppSec, and cloud security tracks. Full training day on binary exploitation.
whyAttend: Hands-on binary exploitation training from working red teamers. Hard content, rare in India.
comfortNote: Hallway track is the real event — most attendees come solo.

title: HackJaipur 2026
description: Jaipur's annual 24-hour hackathon. Open tracks with sponsor challenges from Polygon, Solana, and Vercel.
whyAttend: Sponsor prizes from Polygon/Solana/Vercel on top of the main pool — lots of ways to win.
comfortNote: Team-matching on day zero — come solo, leave with teammates.

title: The Fifth Elephant 2026 — Data & ML
description: India's largest data and ML conference. Tracks on LLM ops, feature stores, experimentation platforms, and applied ML at Indian scale.
whyAttend: Applied ML at Indian scale — the only conference where you'll hear about ML infra at 1B-user products.
comfortNote: Small, casual crowd — easy to start a conversation.

title: Figma Config 2026 — Bengaluru Watch Party
description: Live watch party for Figma Config keynotes, with local designers sharing reactions and takeaways.
whyAttend: Watch Config together — way more fun than alone, plus you meet every designer in BLR.
comfortNote: Friendly for first-timers — most people come alone too.

title: Chennai AI — Fine-Tuning Open Models
description: Hands-on session on fine-tuning Llama-family models with LoRA and QLoRA on consumer GPUs. Bring your laptop.
whyAttend: Walk out knowing how to fine-tune Llama on your own laptop.
comfortNote: Friendly for first-timers — most people come alone too.

title: Pune Fintech Hack 2026
description: 48-hour fintech hackathon with tracks on UPI innovation, lending, and fraud detection. Sponsored by Razorpay and HDFC Bank. ₹10L in total prizes.
whyAttend: ₹10L in prizes and direct access to Razorpay / HDFC product teams.
comfortNote: Team-matching on day zero — come solo, leave with teammates.

title: JSNation 2026 (Virtual)
description: The JavaScript conference. 50+ speakers, online-first, IST-friendly replay.
whyAttend: 50+ speakers covering the whole JS ecosystem. Free tier includes everything worth watching.
comfortNote: Virtual, low-pressure — turn camera off if you want.

title: WordCamp India 2026
description: Two-day WordPress conference with tracks for developers, designers, and site owners. Contributor day on day zero.
whyAttend: Contributor day is gold for first-time open-source contributors — mentors walk you through your first PR.
comfortNote: Friendly for first-timers — most people come alone too.

title: Kubernetes Community Days Bengaluru
description: Full-day community conference on Kubernetes and cloud native. Tracks on platform engineering, observability, and running K8s at Indian-scale cost constraints.
whyAttend: Indian-scale cost optimization content — stuff you won't hear at US-centric K8s talks.
comfortNote: Small, casual crowd — easy to start a conversation.

title: Seeds Delhi — Product People Meetup
description: Casual product meetup for PMs, designers, and founders. Two 15-minute case studies each month plus structured mingling.
whyAttend: Low-pressure product community in Delhi — best place to find your first PM mentor.
comfortNote: Structured mingling = nobody's awkward. Pay what you can.

title: Claude Builders Bengaluru — Agents & Computer Use
description: Monthly meetup for developers building with Claude. This edition: agentic loops, computer use API deep dive, and cost optimization patterns. Hosted by Anthropic's India community team.
whyAttend: Straight from the Anthropic team — rare chance to get unfiltered answers on production Claude usage.
comfortNote: Small room, Anthropic team mingles openly — easy to walk up and ask anything.

title: OpenAI DevDay Delhi Watch Party
description: Community-organized watch party for OpenAI DevDay 2026 keynote. Post-keynote panel with local developers on implementation patterns.
whyAttend: Best way to understand DevDay announcements — watch with other builders who'll interpret it in real time.
comfortNote: Watch parties are the easiest solo event — everyone's watching the same screen.

title: GDG Cloud Bengaluru — Gemini API Hands-On
description: Hands-on workshop building multimodal apps with Gemini API. Laptops required. Google engineers on-site to help.
whyAttend: First Gemini API workshop in BLR — leave having shipped a multimodal demo.
comfortNote: Friendly for first-timers — most people come alone too.

title: Product Management Beginners Workshop — Mumbai
description: 3-hour intro to product management for students and early-career folks. Covers discovery, metrics, PRDs, and how to break into PM roles in India.
whyAttend: If you're a student trying to break into PM, this is the cheapest good intro in Mumbai.
comfortNote: Friendly for first-timers — most people come alone too.

title: SmashingConf Online — CSS & Layout
description: Two days of advanced CSS, container queries, and modern layout techniques. Fully online, IST-friendly sessions in the evening.
whyAttend: Advanced CSS done right — the only place you'll see complete container queries walkthroughs.
comfortNote: Virtual, low-pressure — turn camera off if you want.

title: HackHyderabad Student Hackathon
description: Student-only 24-hour hackathon run by student communities across Hyderabad colleges. Main track + sponsor tracks. Team size 2–4.
whyAttend: Student-only means no pro teams from companies — much fairer shot at winning.
comfortNote: Team-matching on day zero — come solo, leave with teammates.

title: Tech Women India — Career Pivots Panel
description: Panel with 4 women who pivoted into tech mid-career. Discussion on transferable skills, interview prep, and finding mentors. Open to all genders.
whyAttend: Real stories, not LinkedIn-flavored platitudes. Especially if you're considering a pivot into tech.
comfortNote: Virtual, low-pressure — turn camera off if you want.

title: DevRelCon India 2026
description: India's conference for developer relations. Tracks on community building, docs, DevRel measurement, and DevEd. Mix of talks and unconference.
whyAttend: The DevRel community is small and close-knit — this is where jobs get filled.
comfortNote: Small, casual crowd — easy to start a conversation.

title: PWA India — Service Workers & Offline-First
description: Two deep-dive talks on service workers, Background Sync, and patterns for building offline-first PWAs. Includes a live debugging session.
whyAttend: Offline-first patterns are critical for Indian internet conditions — actual applicable content.
comfortNote: Hallway track is the real event — most attendees come solo.

title: Climate Hack India 2026
description: Virtual 48-hour hackathon on climate tech. Challenges from Indian climate NGOs + startups. Open to students and working devs worldwide.
whyAttend: Solve real problems with real NGOs — your work actually gets used, not shelved.
comfortNote: Team-matching on day zero — come solo, leave with teammates.

title: Beginner Python Bootcamp — Pune
description: One-day beginner bootcamp covering Python basics through to building a simple web scraper. Zero prior programming experience assumed.
whyAttend: If you've never coded before, this is the gentlest first step in Pune. ₹299.
comfortNote: Friendly for first-timers — most people come alone too.

title: ML Engineer Interview Prep (Virtual Bootcamp)
description: Two-week async bootcamp on ML interview prep. Daily problem sets, weekly live sessions with ML engineers from Indian startups.
whyAttend: Structured prep beats random LeetCode — cheapest guided ML interview prep available.
comfortNote: Virtual, low-pressure — turn camera off if you want.

title: Open Source Saturday Bengaluru
description: Monthly co-working day for open source contributions. Bring a project, find a project, or get help making your first PR. Mentors on-site.
whyAttend: First-PR mentors walk you through your first open-source contribution. Rare and valuable.
comfortNote: Mentors are specifically there for first-timers — zero judgment.

title: JSLovers Bengaluru — TypeScript Deep Dive
description: Two talks on advanced TypeScript: type-level programming and building DSLs. Plus a 30-minute community showcase for side projects.
whyAttend: Advanced TS content is rare in person in India — this is the only regular venue for it.
comfortNote: Small, casual crowd — easy to start a conversation.

title: UX India 2026
description: India's largest UX conference. Two days, four tracks on research, service design, UX writing, and systems. Workshop day optional.
whyAttend: The one UX conference with real senior speakers in India. Student tickets 60% off.
comfortNote: Hallway track is the real event — most attendees come solo.

title: Web Vitals Workshop — Delhi (Hands-On)
description: 3-hour hands-on workshop on Core Web Vitals optimization. Bring your own live site and we'll profile and fix it together.
whyAttend: Walk in with a slow site, walk out with it faster. Best type of workshop.
comfortNote: Small, casual crowd — easy to start a conversation.

title: Smart India Hackathon 2026 — Registrations Open
description: National-level student hackathon organized by the Ministry of Education. Problem statements from 50+ ministries. Team registration via your college SPOC.
whyAttend: ₹1L per team in prizes + huge resume credibility. Registration closes in days.
comfortNote: Team-matching on day zero — come solo, leave with teammates.

title: Cursor & AI Coding Tools Meetup — Bengaluru
description: First Bengaluru meetup for developers using Cursor, Claude Code, and other AI coding tools. Show-and-tell of workflows, plus a 20-min panel on what actually works.
whyAttend: Everyone's using these tools — this is where you'll see prompts and workflows that actually 10x your work.
comfortNote: Most people come solo — the format (workflow swap) forces low-stakes 1:1 conversations.

title: Freshers Resume Review Night (Online)
description: 90-minute online session where recruiters from Flipkart, Razorpay, and Postman review 10 freshers' resumes live. Submit yours in advance for a chance to be reviewed.
whyAttend: Even if your resume doesn't get picked, watching 10 live reviews teaches you 10x more than any guide.
comfortNote: Virtual and low-pressure — turn your camera off, lurk in chat if you want.
